key_insight: The paper shows that enforcing symmetry and geometric consistency in convolutional autoencoders improves latent stability and makes reduced-order models more reliable.
engineering_relevance: For PDE surrogate modeling, the proposed symmetric CAE architecture can deliver more accurate, robust low-dimensional ROMs than classical convolutional autoencoders.
method: Extend representation-consistent autoencoder ideas from fully connected networks to convolutional layers.
Design symmetric CAEs to better preserve manifold parametrization properties in the latent space.
Integrate the encoder-decoder model into a ROM workflow for parametric PDE dynamics.
evidence_signal: The approach is evaluated on three 1D benchmark PDEs—Linear Advection, Viscous Burger, and Kuramoto–Sivashinsky—and the symmetric CAE consistently outperforms classical CAEs in latent trajectory accuracy, reconstruction error, and robustness.
watch_item: The results are demonstrated on 1D academic test cases, so transfer to higher-dimensional or highly complex PDE settings still needs validation.
paper_title: Convolutional Symmetric AutoEncoders: enhancing latent stability via differential geometry
